title: The Tool Decathlon: Benchmarking Language Agents for Diverse, Realistic, and Long-Horizon Task Execution
summary: Real-world language agents must handle complex, multi-step workflows across diverse Apps. For instance, an agent may manage emails by coordinating with calendars and file systems, or monitor a production database to detect anomalies and generate reports following an operating manual. However, existing language agent benchmarks often focus on narrow domains or simplified tasks that lack the diversity, realism, and long-horizon complexity required to evaluate agents' real-world performance. To address this gap, we introduce the Tool Decathlon (dubbed as Toolathlon), a benchmark for language agents offering diverse Apps and tools, realistic environment setup, and reliable execution-based evaluation. Toolathlon spans 32 software applications and 604 tools, ranging from everyday platforms such as Google Calendar and Notion to professional ones like WooCommerce, Kubernetes, and BigQuery. Most of the tools are based on a high-quality set of Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers that we may have revised or implemented ourselves. Unlike prior works, which primarily ensure functional realism but offer limited environment state diversity, we provide realistic initial environment states from real software, such as Canvas courses with dozens of students or real financial spreadsheets. This benchmark includes 108 manually sourced or crafted tasks in total, requiring interacting with multiple Apps over around 20 turns on average to complete. Each task is strictly verifiable through dedicated evaluation scripts. Comprehensive evaluation of SOTA models highlights their significant shortcomings: the best-performing model, Claude-4.5-Sonnet, achieves only a 38.6% success rate with 20.2 tool calling turns on average, while the top open-weights model DeepSeek-V3.2-Exp reaches 20.1%. We expect Toolathlon to drive the development of more capable language agents for real-world, long-horizon task execution.

AI.tldr: Toolathlon是一个包含32个应用程序和604个工具的语言代理基准，旨在评估代理在真实世界、长周期任务中的性能。
AI.motivation: 现有语言代理基准在狭窄的领域或简化的任务上，缺乏真实性和长周期复杂性来评估真实世界性能。
AI.method: Toolathlon提供了多样化的应用程序和工具，真实的环境设置和基于执行的评估。它跨越32个应用程序和604个工具，包括日常和专业平台。大多数工具基于高质量的MCP服务器。我们提供了来自真实软件的真实初始环境状态，例如包含大量学生或真实金融电子表格的Canvas课程。该基准包含108个手动收集或制作的任务，平均需要大约20次交互才能完成，并通过专门的评估脚本进行严格验证。
AI.result: 在对SOTA模型的全面评估中，表现最好的模型Claude-4.5-Sonnet的成功率为38.6%，平均工具调用次数为20.2次，而顶尖的开源模型DeepSeek-V3.2-Exp的成功率为20.1%。
AI.conclusion: 我们期望Toolathlon能够推动开发更强大的语言代理，以应对真实世界中的长周期任务执行。

title: Benchmark Dataset Generation and Evaluation for Excel Formula Repair with LLMs
comment: Accepted at the KDD workshop on Evaluation and Trustworthiness of Agentic and Generative AI Models
summary: Excel is a pervasive yet often complex tool, particularly for novice users, where runtime errors arising from logical mistakes or misinterpretations of functions pose a significant challenge. While large language models (LLMs) offer promising assistance by explaining formula errors, the automated correction of these semantic runtime errors remains an open problem. A primary challenge to advancing models for such scenarios is the severe lack of high-quality, comprehensive datasets for training and rigorous evaluation. This paper addresses this gap by introducing a novel approach for constructing a benchmark dataset specifically designed for Excel formula repair. We propose a data generation pipeline, which leverages a small set of curated seed samples from online forums to synthetically expand the dataset. Our pipeline integrates few-shot prompting with LLMs and employs a robust \textit{LLM-as-a-Judge} validation framework, combined with execution-based checks to ensure the correctness and semantic fidelity of the generated data. This process produced a benchmark dataset of 618 high-quality samples, covering common runtime errors. Furthermore, we propose a context-aware baseline technique for Excel formula repair that utilizes LLMs to leverage both the faulty formula, and relevant spreadsheet context. We evaluate the performance of various LLMs (GPT-4o, GPT-4.1, Phi-3, Mistral) on our newly generated benchmark using execution-based metrics. Our analysis demonstrates the dataset's quality through manual annotation and provides insights into error and function distributions. The proposed generation methodology is highly scalable and can be readily adapted to create evaluation benchmarks for similar code repair tasks in other low-resource programming languages.

AI.tldr: 该研究提出了一个用于Excel公式修复的数据集和基线方法，以解决新手用户在处理Excel公式时遇到的常见运行时错误。
AI.motivation: Excel新手用户常因逻辑错误或函数误解导致运行时错误，现有LLM虽能解释错误但无法自动修复，且缺乏高质量的训练和评估数据集。
AI.method: 提出数据生成流程，利用在线论坛的种子样本，结合LLM的少样本提示学习和LLM-as-a-Judge验证框架，以及基于执行的检查，生成包含618个样本的数据集。并提出一个利用LLM结合错误公式和电子表格上下文的基线修复技术。
AI.result: 生成了一个包含618个高质量样本的数据集，覆盖了常见的运行时错误。在评估中，使用执行指标测试了GPT-4o、GPT-4.1、Phi-3、Mistral等LLM在新建基准上的表现，并进行了人工标注和错误/函数分布分析。
AI.conclusion: 该研究填补了Excel公式修复领域高质量数据集的空白，提出的数据生成方法具有良好的可扩展性，可用于其他低资源编程语言的代码修复任务。
